title: phragmites communis var. stolonifera
intro: Phragmites communis var. stolonifera, also known as sweet cane or common reed, is a type of wetland grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Phragmites communis var. stolonifera include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health, as well as its potential to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues.
